articles.title: Record rain breaks gruelling heatwave in Ludhiana. - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Punjab
articles.full_text:
Ludhiana: A state-record downpour has broken a fierce summer heatwave, sending temperatures plummeting nearly 13 degrees below seasonal averages and providing the region with desperate relief.
The district emerged as the wettest location in
Punjab after receiving 32.6 mm of rain, says the India Meteorological Department (IMD). The steady, hours-long deluge drastically improved local air quality, suppressed heavy dust levels, and drew crowds back into public parks and markets after weeks of oppressive heat.
The IMD recorded a maximum temperature of just 26.4°C — a stark 12.9 degrees below the seasonal norm — while the minimum temperature hovered at 24.2°C. However, meteorologists warn the respite will be short-lived. A gradual warming trend is already underway, with the mercury expected to hit 35°C on Sunday before climbing to a scorching 40°C by June 5.
By Friday evening, skies had largely cleared to allow sunshine back into the city. While humidity levels spiked significantly following the rain, prevailing cool winds ensured conditions remained comfortable into the night.
